summary: coral-wraith postinstall script exfiltrates host information over HTTP
details: The package's postinstall.js executes automatically on npm install and pulls in os, fs, http, and child_process. It collects host identifiers (multiple os.hostname() calls at lines 34, 65, 83), reads files from the filesystem (fs.readFileSync at line 87), and issues outbound HTTP requests (http.request at lines 49 and 69, plus GET operations at lines 6 and 95) to transmit the gathered data. This combination of system-info collection and network transmission inside a lifecycle script that fires on default install is the canonical install-time exfiltration shape and produces direct attacker benefit (host fingerprinting + filesystem content leakage from any machine that runs npm install).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
